title: Big Protests in America
summary: This article talks about a big protest called 'No Kings' that happened in America. People all over the country, in all 50 states, came together to march and hold signs. They were upset with President Trump and his government's rules. The protest was very large, with millions of people joining in. Organizers say it was the biggest single-day protest in U.S. history. People are frustrated about things like immigration and wars. They feel tired but hopeful that by gathering together, they can make changes. The article also mentions experts, Amy Walter and Tamara Keith, who discuss politics and what these protests mean for elections.

keywords.explanation: when people gather to show they are unhappy about something
people who plan and run an event like a protest
feeling upset or annoyed when things are not going well
the group of people who make rules and run a country
feeling positive that good things will happen in the future
people who know a lot about a topic, like politics

questions.correct_answer: It was the largest single-day protest in U.S. history

questions.question: What feeling did people have that gave them hope?
Who are Amy Walter and Tamara Keith?

background_read: In America, people sometimes hold protests to share their feelings about government rules. Protests can be big events where many people march with signs. This helps them speak out and try to make changes. Experts like Amy Walter and Tamara Keith study politics to understand what these events mean for elections and the country.
Article_Structure: WHO: People in America, including protest organizers and experts Amy Walter and Tamara Keith
WHAT: A big protest called 'No Kings' where millions marched against President Trump's policies
WHERE: In all 50 states of the U.S. and even in other countries
WHY: Because people are frustrated with government rules on immigration and wars, and they want to create change together

perspectives.description: They feel frustrated and tired but hopeful that gathering can lead to change.
